MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-09-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER JO RICCOMI'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO SEPTEMBER 19, 2014. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =302/EHG
ORDER RE: PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PETITIONER JO RICCOMI'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS - THE COURT GRANTS RESPONDENT'S REQUEST TO AUGMENT THE RECORD PURSUANT TO CCP 1094.5(E). THE COURT DENIES THE PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. FIRST, THE 90-DAY DECISION PERIOD OF SECTION 216.8 OF THE MOU IS DIRECTORY AND RESPONDENT'S FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THAT TIME LIMITATION DOES NOT WARRANT MANDAMUS RELIEF. SEE BETTENCOURT V. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO (2007) 146 CAL.APP.4TH 1090, 1102. SECOND, RESPONDENT HAD THE AUTHORITY TO SUSPEND PETITIONER AND PLACE HER ON PROBATION PURSUANT TO SECTIONS 212.2 AND 213.2 OF THE MOU. THIRD, PETITIONER FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE OTHER HIGHLIGHTED PROCEDURAL ERRORS, WHICH RESPONDENT TOOK ACCOUNT OF WHEN IT REDUCED THE PENALTY AGAINST PETITIONER, WERE PREJUDICIAL OR MANDATE MANDAMUS RELIEF. THE COURT STRIKES PETITIONER'S SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY BRIEF. THAT BRIEF RAISES NEW ISSUES THAT THE MOVING POINTS AND AUTHORITIES DO NOT ADDRESS AND THE COURT DID NOT GRANT PETITIONER LEAVE TO FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Sep-19-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. WILLIAM H. SORTOR OF CLISHAM & SORTOR, TEL. NO. 291-0855 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. MICHAEL J. VARTAN OF THE VARTAIN LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 391-1155 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER JO RICCOMI's Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus - The court grants respondent's request to augment the record pursuant to CCP 1094.5(e). The court denies the petition for writ of mandate. First, the 90-day decision period of section 216.8 of the MOU is directory and respondent's failure to comply with that time limitation does not warrant mandamus relief. See Bettencourt v. City and County of San Francisco (2007) 146 Cal.App.4th 1090, 1102. Second, respondent had the authority to suspend petitioner and place her on probation pursuant to sections 212.2 and 213.2 of the MOU. Third, petitioner fails to show that the other highlighted procedural errors, which respondent took account of when it reduced the penalty against petitioner, were prejudicial or mandate mandamus relief. The court strikes petitioner's supplemental reply brief. That brief raises new issues that the moving points and authorities do not address and the court did not grant petitioner leave to file a supplemental brief. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg
